description:
Plumbing landed (f46dd968, fc1ff766). Force-drop attempt failed (e4e3aafe, reverted as 98e5e5a7).

## Current state in tree

- Descr::arrow_join_return helper.
- Typer cont_slot0_descr handles Term::CallClosure via arrow_join_return.
- Closure header packs halt_kind (high 2 bits of flags).
- fz_alloc_closure / init_static_closures / fz_aot_register_static_closure / fz_spawn_entry thread halt_kind end-to-end.
- chain_repr / pure_forwarder analysis (computed but not used to drop the force).

## Why the force-drop broke apply2

apply2(f, x), do: f(x) lowers to Term::TailCallClosure on apply2's first arg (not a capture). chain_repr looks up the closure Var's Descr via spec_fn_types[sid].vars; for apply2's spec key [(any) -> any, 21], f's Descr is (any) -> any. arrow_join_return gives any → Tagged. apply2's chain_repr = Tagged.

The TailCallClosure terminator seed still flagged apply2 in tagged_return_fns → apply2's return_reprs forced Tagged → apply2's outer @abi = ... -> Tagged. But double's body (post force-drop) returned RawInt raw. return_call_indirect can't coerce mid-TCO → apply2 forwarded raw bits up under a Tagged ABI. main read Tagged from raw 42, sshr → 5.

## Two fix paths

(α) Per-callsite spec specialization on closure-arg types. apply2 gets a narrow spec [(int) -> int, 21] where f's Descr is (int) -> int. chain_repr extracts int → RawInt. apply2's narrow spec returns RawInt. main resolves to the narrow spec and reads RawInt. This is exactly fz-ul4.27.22.7.

(β) Keep closure_target_fns force in tagged_return_fns. Bodies return Tagged → all forwarders consistent. Caps the win at: closure capture-slot narrowing (22.5) + halt_kind plumbing for spawn (22.6 partial).

## Recommendation

Do 22.7 (path α). It generalizes: arg-slot narrowing and return-slot narrowing both fall out of per-callsite spec specialization. Once narrow specs exist for closure-arg types, the force-drop is naturally safe — apply2's narrow spec returns RawInt by construction; main reads RawInt; no mid-TCO coerce needed.

The plumbing already in tree (chain_repr, pure_forwarder, halt_kind in closure header) is the right foundation. 22.7 lands the missing piece: typer-side closure-arg spec specialization.
title: fz-cps-seam.return: closure body return-slot narrowing via typer cont_slot0_descr
